records.specific_details: Wrote to the Joint Committee on Disability Matters to express urgent concern regarding the challenges faced by persons with disabilities living within the Direct Provision system.
records.intended_results: Request that the Committee considers and discusses the concerns and takes corrective action urgently
lobbying_activities: Collaborative letter co-signed by a collective of advocates, organisations and academics - Letter

records.lobbyist_name: Athlone Chamber of Commerce and Industry
records.specific_details: Support for Athlone 2040 project
records.intended_results: To express the Chamber’s strong support for the Athlone 2040 project, an ambitious plan to grow Athlone into a sustainable, 100,000-population, 15-minute green city by 2040. We sought to reinforce political backing for the initiative, highlight its potential for economic and community transformation, and encourage continued commitment and investment from national and local representatives.

records.specific_details: Wrote to Oireachtas Committee Members Regarding 1988 Act prior to appearing before Committee
records.intended_results: Pre-legislative scrutiny of any amendments to the Housing Act 1988 with a focus on compliance with human rights. Letter sent prior to appearance before sitting of committee discussing homelessness.
lobbying_activities: Wrote to all members of the Joint Oireachtas Committee for Housing, Local Government, and Heritage to inform them of previous engagements with Department of Housing on issues related to the Housing Act 1988. Informed them of concerns related to potential amendments and impact on human rights. Requested Pre-Legislative Scrutiny and engagement with NGO sector. - Email

Wrote to all members of the Joint Oireachtas Committee for Housing, Local Government, and Heritage to inform them of previous engagements with Department of Housing on issues related to the Housing Act 1988. Informed them of concerns related to potential amendments and impact on human rights. Requested Pre-Legislative Scrutiny and engagement with NGO sector. - Letter

records.intended_results: To foster engagement with key business and political stakeholders by inviting them to the Chamber’s Corporate Lunch, aiming to strengthen relationships, support dialogue on economic priorities, and highlight issues of strategic importance to Athlone’s business community.

records.specific_details: Pre-Budget Communication to the Seanad, Ministers and TD's highlighting the issues facing people with intellectual disabilities and their families, and the critical sustainability issues that are facing the organisations providing supports for these individuals - this includes St Michael's House.
records.intended_results: In Budget 2025, it is essential that the Government commits to fully resourcing and implementing the Action Plan for Disability Services 2024-2026.

records.specific_details: Input into Housing Committee Work Programme
records.intended_results: Sought for the Housing Committee to prioritise sessions on implementing minimum BER standards in private rental housing from 2025, including timeline, enforcement and tenant protections against renovictions, and on scaling up local authority and AHB retrofit programmes with multi-annual funding and a focus on worst-performing stock, to ensure climate targets are met while protecting tenants and reducing energy poverty.
lobbying_activities: Called for the Housing Committee to hold sessions on:Implementation of minimum BER standards in private rental housing from 2025, including timeline, enforcement, and tenant protections against renovictions.Scaling up local authority and AHB retrofit programmes, with a focus on prioritising the worst-performing stock and shifting to multi-annual funding.Provided supporting evidence from ESRI analysis, Department consultations, and NGO research. - Email
Met with Deputy Hearne outlining the same - Meeting

records.specific_details: The Wheel hosted launch event for our Pre-Budget Submission 2026. It comprised a panel discussion with speakers from community and voluntary organisations and an overview of The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026. We invited Ministers, TDs and Senators from the most relevant departments and joint Oireachtas committees and several attended in person.
records.intended_results: To inform TDs and Senators about The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026, which includes issues such as sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.

records.specific_details: IFA Farmer Health Check 2025
records.intended_results: To provide Farmers across the country with free health checks with the continued support of the Department of Agriculture, Food, and the Marine.::To build on the success of last year's rollout, the health checks include blood pressure monitoring, pulse and rhythm screening, BMI assessments, and lifestyle advice — all delivered by the expert nursing team from Croí.::To emphasise that the health of farmers is just as critical as the productivity of their land and livestock. IFA is proud to partner with Teagasc and Croí for this event to ensure that farmer health and wellbeing remain a priority. These checks are quick, confidential and could be lifesaving.

records.specific_details: On behalf of members to make a pre-budget submission
records.intended_results: Raising awareness of the Building Life campaign, the aim of which is to achieve a broad range of private sector actions and public policy changes that will be required to tackle the whole-life environmental impact of the built environment.

records.lobbyist_name: Society of St. Vincent de Paul
records.specific_details: Retrofitting Supports for energy Poverty
records.intended_results: To inform elected reps of the need for enhanced retrofitting supports for people in energy poverty a session dedicated to energy poverty.
lobbying_activities: Call for JOC to dedicate a session to energy poverty. - Letter

records.specific_details: The Wheel's Pre-Budget Submission 2026 was sent to elected representatives and relevant government departments in advance of the Budget. It highlights the priorities for the community and voluntary sector, including sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.
records.intended_results: To inform elected representatives and relevant departments about the priorities for the community and voluntary sector in relation to Budget 2026.

records.lobbyist_name: National Forum of Family Resource Centres CLG
records.specific_details: The Family Resource Centre National Forum held a political engagement event on the 28th of May in Dublin.
records.intended_results: Raise profile of the National FRC Programmeand provided an opportunity for Oireachtasmembers to meet with FRC representativesfrom their region.Launch of the FRC National Forum Pre-Budget Submission for 2026 includingseeking an optimum core funding of €240,000for all 121 Family Resource Centres, a capital funding stream for FRCs and funding for counselling services.
